title: UAV Structural Components Manufacturing
entry_mechanism: Turkish-Saudi JV under Baykar co-production framework
executive_summary:
Saudi Arabia's most visible near-term UAV co-production commitment centres on the Baykar Akinci MALE-class twin-turboprop system. SAMI CEO Walid Abukhaled has publicly committed to building the Akinci airframe in-Kingdom — a statement that directly defines this node's mandate. MA-1 is the structural manufacturing capability that makes that commitment real: composite and aluminium sub-assemblies, including wing structural elements, fuselage panels, control surfaces, and structural fittings, produced in Saudi Arabia to Baykar's technical acceptance standard.

The global composite UAV airframe market was valued at $1.85 billion in 2024 and is projected to reach $4.81 billion by 2031 at a CAGR of 16.5%. Saudi Arabia's own UAV demand — anchored by the Akinci programme and future sovereign MALE requirements under GADD — provides a captive domestic demand base that de-risks the investment.

This node is Phase I priority because it directly enables the Akinci co-production timeline, builds composite manufacturing capability that transfers to commercial aerospace (Node CA-2) and future military aircraft programmes, and establishes Saudi Arabia's first credible Tier 2 aerostructures position.

companies.name: Baykar
companies.rationale: Mandatory partner holding the Akinci TDP and existing MOU with SAMI.
companies.contact: SAMI VP Programmes → Baykar Head of International Co-production

companies.name: Sonaca
companies.rationale: Tier 1/2 aerostructures with active Gulf interest; interim partner if Baykar MLA delayed.
companies.contact: Sonaca VP Business Development, Middle East & Africa

companies.name: GKN Aerospace
companies.rationale: World's largest independent aerostructures Tier 1; F-35, A350, 787 pedigree; existing Saudi engagement.
companies.contact: GKN Aerospace VP Strategy and M&A

companies.name: Daher
companies.rationale: French Tier 2 with strong technology transfer track record; more agile JV partner than large primes.

companies.name: Turkish Aerospace Industries (TAI)
companies.rationale: Direct insight into Baykar supply chain standards; existing SAMI relationship through T129 programme.

partners.name: SAMI Composites LLC
partners.current: Basic composite fabrication, limited autoclave access
partners.upgrade: AS9100 programme + autoclave investment + Baykar secondment + 25 Saudi operator training

partners.name: SABIC Composites
partners.current: Carbon fibre precursor and polymer matrix R&D, materials supply
partners.upgrade: Local prepreg supply agreement + embedded R&D team in JV + Baykar material qualification

partners.name: KFUPM Materials Engineering
partners.current: Composite fabrication research, NDT lab, materials testing
partners.upgrade: GADD-KFUPM training programme + industrial placement + NDT lab certification

partners.name: Al-Rushaid Group
partners.current: Heavy fabrication, CNC machining for oil and gas
partners.upgrade: 5-axis CNC upgrade + AS9100 Tier 3 qualification + chemical processing line
